calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-23-2026 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-11-2026 Continued To May-20-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEFAULT HEARING (PROVE UP)
Off Calendar Apr-01-2026 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: Court, Counsel for Plaintiff, and its witness Patty Montanez, Credit Manager with Linkedin are present.

09:17 a.m. -

This matter comes on calendar being set for motion for prove-up hearing.

Dept. 514; Judge: Daniel A. Flores; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 09:17:03 a.m. and concluding at 10:10:50 a.m.

Court has reviewed the minutes from hearing on Feb-25-2026 and determines that it left off with procedural matters regarding this action. Jurisdiction was at issue in this case and nothing has been filed by Plaintiff about whether this should have been brought in Santa Clara County or San Francisco.

Plaintiff intends to proceed and argues that Defendant resides in San Francisco.
Counsel for Plaintiff presents oral argument on Why this Court should proceed without compliance of its previous orders.

09:22 a.m. - The matter submitted.

This matter came on calendar on Feb-25-2026 at 09:00 a.m. for default and the moving party was present. Plaintiff was represented by George Aposhian, Esq. and Plaintiff's witness was unavailable.

Court reads minutes from Feb-25-2026 into the record.
COURT DISMISSES ENTIRE ACTION WITHOUT PREJUDICE without making rulings on the merit. Court refers to and reads Paragraph 10 of the Contract into the record. Court makes closing remarks and adjourns.

DEFAULT HEARING (PROVE UP)
Off Calendar Feb-25-2026 Continued To Feb-25-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 514

DEFAULT HEARING (PROVE UP)
Off Calendar Feb-25-2026 Continued To Apr-01-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 514
MINI MINUTES: Dept. 514; Judge Daniel A. Flores; Clerk: Shanika Monroe;Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 09:40:59 a.m. and concluding at 09:47:07 a.m.

This matter comes on calendar being set for default prove-up hearing.

Plaintiff Creditors Adjustment Bureau, Inc. represented by George Aposhian, Esq. via Zoom Video Conference.

Defendant Kyte Systems, Inc. AKA Kyte not appearing.

Plaintiff's witness is unavailable.
Court determines that Counsel for Plaintiff has not complied with Local Rule 6.6 (D)(7)(b), which requires that there be a request filed to excuse a witness from trial.

Court has reviewed the pleadings that were submitted for the hearing and notes that the contract in this case is enforceable in Santa Clara County. The matter likely should have been brought in Santa Clara County.

Court further notes that Counsel for Plaintiff did not include the amount of time worked on the case in connection with its request for attorney's fees. Counsel to address both issues before the next hearing.

Court orders matter continued to Apr-01-2026 at 09:00 a.m. in Department 514 pursuant to the request of Plaintiff.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-29-2026 Continued To Apr-08-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-29-2025 Continued To Feb-25-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
